title: Banh Mi-Style Chicken Bowls with Pickled Veggies & Sriracha Mayo
description: EveryPlate fan favorite — all the bold flavors of a Vietnamese banh mi in a rice bowl. Lemongrass-marinated chicken, quick-pickled carrots and daikon, fresh cucumber, cilantro, and a creamy sriracha mayo over jasmine rice.
instructions: Cook rice according to package directions.
Make the quick pickles: julienne or grate carrots and daikon. Toss with rice vinegar, sugar, and a pinch of salt. Let sit at least 15 minutes.
Make sriracha mayo: stir together mayonnaise, sriracha, and a squeeze of lime juice. Set aside.
Marinate chicken: mix soy sauce, fish sauce, minced garlic, and a squeeze of lime juice. Toss with chicken thighs and let sit 10 minutes.
Heat vegetable oil in a skillet over medium-high heat. Cook chicken 5–6 minutes per side until cooked through and caramelized. Let rest 3 minutes, then slice.
Divide rice into bowls. Arrange sliced chicken, pickled carrots and daikon, cucumber, and jalapeño on top.
Drizzle generously with sriracha mayo. Top with fresh cilantro and a squeeze of lime.
tips: Fish sauce is the key to authentic banh mi flavor — don't skip it. It adds depth without tasting fishy.
The pickled vegetables can be made up to 3 days ahead and stored in the fridge.
Chicken thighs stay juicier than breasts for this preparation, but breasts work too.
For a more authentic touch, add a few sprigs of fresh mint alongside the cilantro.
